batch: Batch143 — P0/P1 Final Closure Candidate & Visual Smoke Contract Harmonization
purpose: Push P0/P1 as far as possible without overclaiming: align visual smoke viewport contracts, require real Chromium screenshot capture for hosted visual evidence, and keep public rollout blocked until Node24 CI/APP_URL/save-export/visual/P0-100 proof all pass.
strictProofRequires: Node 24 GitHub Actions/Vercel runtime proof with CI provenance
visual smoke evidence at required breakpoints
final p0-100 release chain with separate artifact contract
canonical visual viewport ids shared by template, validator, hosted CI report, public rollout and P0/P1 stability gates

requiredEvidence.label: Node24 GitHub Actions runtime proof with provenance

mustShow: sidebar/bottom navigation does not trap the screen

localOrSourcePassCanClaim: Batch142 source-level CI provenance hardening is present
Local Node24 runs can no longer close the Node24 CI/Vercel evidence item
public rollout still blocked until real GitHub Actions Node24, APP_URL hosted smoke, save/export smoke, visual smoke and P0-100 artifacts pass

artifactIntegrityRules: Hosted strict release and P0-100 release must use separate artifact files.
A required evidence item is pass only when artifact.ok=true and the artifact proofProfile/command/required flags match that evidence item.
verify-release-strict cannot satisfy p0_100_release because it lacks requireVisualSmoke=true and requireNode24=true.
P0-100 release must include Node24, hosted URL and visual smoke requirements before public rollout can be closed.
Node24 deepest CI proof cannot be satisfied by a local Node24 run; artifact.ciProvenance.githubActions must be true when requireCiProvenance=true.
The node24_ci_deepest evidence item must include GitHub workflow/run provenance before it can unblock hosted/public proof.
Local Node24 proof is useful for debugging only and must remain unverified/blocked for CI/Vercel claims.
Visual smoke evidence viewport ids must use the canonical hyphenated ids so template/capture/validator/public rollout all read the same contract.
